1	\chapter{Flat base change for the pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf ($i=0$)}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_FlatBaseChange}
3	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Section "Cohomology and
4	% base change, I", and Tag 02KH (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex and
5	% references/stacks-coherent.md).
6	
7	\section{Setup and motivation}
8	
9	This chapter establishes the $i=0$ (i.e.\ \(H^0\) / direct-image) case of flat
10	base change: the formation of the pushforward \(f_*\mathcal{F}\) of a
11	quasi-coherent sheaf commutes with flat base change on the target. It is the
12	foundational, fully self-contained slice of the cohomology-and-base-change
13	package that ultimately underlies relative representability statements; the
14	higher derived cases \(R^i f_*\) for \(i \geq 1\) are treated separately and are
15	out of scope here.
16	
17	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes that is quasi-compact and
18	quasi-separated, and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module.
19	A base change of \(f\) along an arbitrary morphism \(g : S' \to S\) is recorded
20	by the cartesian square obtained from the fibre product
21	\(X' = X \times_S S'\):
22	\[
23	  \begin{array}{ccc}
24	    X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
25	    {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
26	    S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
27	  \end{array}
28	\]
29	Here \(f' : X' \to S'\) is the base change of \(f\), and \(g' : X' \to X\) is the
30	first projection. We write \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) for the pullback
31	of \(\mathcal{F}\) to \(X'\).
32	
33	\section{The canonical base-change map}
34	
35	\begin{definition}\leanok
36	[Base-change map for the pushforward]
37	  \label{def:pushforward_base_change_map}
38	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardBaseChangeMap}
39	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Section "Cohomology and
40	  % base change, I", base-change diagram (\ref{equation-base-change-diagram})
41	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L877--904).
42	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
43	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent sheaf on $X$.
44	  % Suppose further that $g : S' \to S$ is any morphism of schemes. Denote
45	  % $X' = X_{S'} = S' \times_S X$ the base change of $X$ and denote
46	  % $f' : X' \to S'$ the base change of $f$.
47	  % Also write $g' : X' \to X$ the projection,
48	  % and set $\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}$."
49	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S\,Cohomology and base
50	  change, I.}
51	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes, \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
52	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and \(g : S' \to S\) an arbitrary morphism, giving the
53	  cartesian square above with projection \(g' : X' \to X\) and
54	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\). The \emph{base-change map for the
55	  pushforward} is the canonical morphism of \(\mathcal{O}_{S'}\)-modules
56	  \[
57	    g^*\bigl(f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \longrightarrow f'_*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr)
58	    = f'_*\mathcal{F}' .
59	  \]
60	  It is adjoint to the morphism
61	  \(f_*\mathcal{F} \to g_*\, f'_*\mathcal{F}' = f_*\,(g')_*\mathcal{F}'\) obtained
62	  by applying \(f_*\) to the unit
63	  \(\mathcal{F} \to (g')_*(g')^*\mathcal{F}\) of the
64	  \(((g')^*, (g')_*)\)-adjunction, using the commutativity
65	  \(g \circ f' = f \circ g'\) of the square.
66	\end{definition}
67	
68	\section{The affine case}
69	
70	\subsection{Locality of isomorphisms for \texttt{Scheme.Modules} morphisms}
71	
72	Before treating the affine computation we record three project-local supplements
73	to Mathlib. Mathlib provides the per-open criterion
74	(\(\mathrm{IsIso}\,\varphi \iff \forall U,\ \mathrm{IsIso}(\varphi_U)\)) and a
75	stalkwise isomorphism criterion for \(\mathrm{TopCat.Sheaf}\)-valued morphisms, but
76	it does not package the stalk-local or basis-local criterion at the level of
77	morphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. The following lemmas bridge that gap; they
78	are exactly the locality tools used in the first reduction of the affine
79	base-change lemma below, where one checks the base-change map after restricting to
80	the affine opens of the base. As bespoke infrastructure they carry no external
81	citation and stand on their proof sketches.
82	
83	\begin{lemma}\leanok
84	[Stalk-local criterion for isomorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules]
85	  \label{lem:modules_isIso_iff_stalk}
86	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_stalkFunctor_map}
87	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\varphi : M \to N\) a morphism of sheaves of
88	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Then \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism if and only if the
89	  underlying morphism of abelian-group presheaves induces an isomorphism on the
90	  stalk at every point \(x \in X\).
91	\end{lemma}
92	
93	\begin{proof}
94	
95	
96	  If \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism, then so is its image under the forgetful
97	  functor to abelian-group presheaves, and the stalk functor at each \(x\),
98	  being a functor, carries isomorphisms to isomorphisms. Conversely, assume the
99	  stalk map is an isomorphism at every \(x\). Package the underlying abelian-group
100	  presheaves of \(M\) and \(N\) as sheaves of abelian groups, and \(\varphi\) as a
101	  morphism of these sheaves; the stalkwise-isomorphism hypothesis lets us apply the
102	  \(\mathrm{TopCat.Sheaf}\)-level stalk-local isomorphism criterion to conclude that
103	  this morphism of sheaves of abelian groups is an isomorphism. Pushing it through
104	  the forgetful functor to presheaves yields an isomorphism of the underlying
105	  abelian-group presheaf morphisms, and since the forgetful functor from
106	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to abelian-group presheaves reflects isomorphisms,
107	  \(\varphi\) itself is an isomorphism.
108	\end{proof}
109	
110	\begin{lemma}\leanok
111	[Basis-local criterion for isomorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules]
112	  \label{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis}
113	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis}
114	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_iff_stalk}
115	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, let \(\{B_i\}_{i \in \iota}\) be a family of opens whose
116	  range is a basis of the topology of \(X\), and let \(\varphi : M \to N\) be a
117	  morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. If \(\varphi\) restricts to an
118	  isomorphism on the sections over every basic open \(B_i\), then \(\varphi\) is an
119	  isomorphism.
120	\end{lemma}
121	
122	\begin{proof}
123	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_iff_stalk}
124	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_stalk} it suffices to prove that the induced
125	  stalk map is bijective at each point \(x \in X\). For injectivity, a germ that
126	  vanishes at \(x\) already vanishes on some open neighbourhood, which may be
127	  shrunk to a basic open \(B_i\); on \(B_i\) the section map \(\varphi_{B_i}\) is
128	  injective, so the original section is zero, giving injectivity of the stalk map
129	  from injectivity over the basis. For surjectivity, an arbitrary germ at \(x\) is
130	  represented by a section of \(N\) over some basic open \(B_i\) containing \(x\);
131	  since \(\varphi_{B_i}\) is surjective, this section lifts to a section of \(M\)
132	  over \(B_i\), whose germ at \(x\) maps to the given germ. Thus the stalk map is
133	  bijective, and \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism.
134	\end{proof}
135	
136	\begin{lemma}\leanok
137	[Affine-open locality criterion for isomorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules]
138	  \label{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}
139	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens}
140	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis}
141	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\varphi : M \to N\) a morphism of sheaves of
142	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Then \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism if and only if it
143	  restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of \(X\).
144	\end{lemma}
145	
146	\begin{proof}
147	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis}
148	  The forward implication is immediate, since the section functor over a fixed open
149	  preserves isomorphisms. For the converse, the affine opens of a scheme form a
150	  basis of its topology, so the claim is the special case of
151	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis} in which the basis is the family of
152	  affine opens.
153	\end{proof}
154	
155	\subsection{The affine computation}
156	
157	The whole result rests on the following elementary computation in the affine
158	setting, where the base-change map is visibly an isomorphism because tensoring is
159	associative. Before stating it we isolate the one ingredient not yet available as
160	a ready-made result on which the affine reduction turns: the description of the
161	pushforward of a tilde-module along a morphism of affine schemes.
162	
163	We first record three bespoke \(\Gamma\)-fragment supports that compute the global
164	sections of an affine pushforward; they carry no external citation and stand on
165	their proof sketches.
166	
167	\begin{lemma}
168	
169	\leanok
170	[Naturality of the global-sections comparison square]
171	  \label{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
172	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
173	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings, and write
174	  \(\mathrm{gs}_R : \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \top) \cong R\) and
175	  \(\mathrm{gs}_{R'} : \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R', \top) \cong R'\) for the
176	  global-sections isomorphisms of the structure sheaves. Then the ring square
177	  \[
178	    \mathrm{gs}_R \;\circ\; (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{\sharp}_{\top}
179	    \;=\; \varphi \;\circ\; \mathrm{gs}_{R'}
180	  \]
181	  commutes, where \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{\sharp}_{\top}\) is the
182	  top-open component of the structure-sheaf comparison map underlying
183	  \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\).
184	\end{lemma}
185	
186	\begin{proof}
187	
188	
189	  This is the naturality of the unit/counit of the
190	  \(\Gamma \dashv \operatorname{Spec}\) adjunction read off at the top open: the
191	  global-sections map of \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) is, under the
192	  global-sections isomorphisms on each side, conjugate to \(\varphi\) itself.
193	  Transposing the naturality square of the \(\operatorname{Spec}\) unit gives
194	  exactly the asserted ring equation.
195	\end{proof}
196	
197	\begin{lemma}
198	
199	\leanok
200	[Global sections of an affine pushforward]
201	  \label{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
202	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardIso}
203	  \uses{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
204	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let
205	  \(N\) be an \(\operatorname{Spec}(R')\)-module. Then there is a canonical
206	  isomorphism of \(R\)-modules
207	  \[
208	    \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N\bigr)
209	    \;\cong\;
210	    \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma N\bigr),
211	  \]
212	  identifying the \(R\)-module of global sections of the pushforward with the
213	  restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\) of the \(R'\)-module of global sections
214	  of \(N\).
215	\end{lemma}
216	
217	\begin{proof}
218	  \uses{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
219	  Because \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}(\top) = \top\), the global sections of
220	  the pushforward \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N\) are, on underlying abelian
221	  groups, the global sections of \(N\) with no transport along the open-set
222	  identification. The two sides therefore carry the same underlying abelian group;
223	  both unwind to nested restriction-of-scalars towers along the structure-sheaf
224	  global-sections maps. These towers are reconciled by applying the
225	  restriction-of-scalars composition identity twice and substituting the ring
226	  equation of
227	  Lemma~\ref{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}, which shows the
228	  resulting \(R\)-action is exactly restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\).
229	\end{proof}
230	
231	\begin{lemma}
232	
233	\leanok
234	[Global sections of an affine pushforward of a tilde-module]
235	  \label{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso}
236	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardTildeIso}
237	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
238	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let \(M\)
239	  be an \(R'\)-module. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of \(R\)-modules
240	  \[
241	    \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \widetilde{M}\bigr)
242	    \;\cong\;
243	    \operatorname{restr}_\varphi M .
244	  \]
245	\end{lemma}
246	
247	\begin{proof}
248	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
249	  Specialise Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso} to \(N = \widetilde{M}\) and
250	  compose with the tilde-unit isomorphism
251	  \(\Gamma(\widetilde{M}) \cong M\) of \(R'\)-modules (which restricts to an
252	  isomorphism of \(R\)-modules after applying \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi\)).
253	\end{proof}
254	
255	\begin{lemma}
256	
257	\leanok
258	[Sections of an affine pushforward over an arbitrary open]
259	  \label{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}
260	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardIsoAt}
261	  \uses{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
262	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings, let \(N\) be
263	  an \(\operatorname{Spec}(R')\)-module, and let \(U\) be an open of
264	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\), with preimage
265	  \(V = (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}(U)\) in \(\operatorname{Spec}(R')\). Then
266	  there is a canonical isomorphism of \(R\)-modules
267	  \[
268	    \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N,\, U\bigr)
269	    \;\cong\;
270	    \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(N,\, V)\bigr) .
271	  \]
272	  This is the open-indexed generalization of
273	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}, which is the case \(U = \top\) (so that
274	  \(V = \top\) as well). The family \(\{e_U\}_U\) of these isomorphisms is moreover
275	  natural in the open argument: the structure-sheaf restriction maps on the two
276	  sides commute with the isomorphisms, so the family is a natural isomorphism of the
277	  two presheaves of \(R\)-modules in the open variable (this naturality is
278	  established in the proof below). For \(U = D(a)\) it is exactly the linear
279	  equivalence \(e_{D(a)}\) of movement~(1) in the proof of
280	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}.
281	\end{lemma}
282	
283	\begin{proof}
284	  \uses{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
285	  The construction copies that of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso} verbatim,
286	  with the top open \(\top\) replaced throughout by the arbitrary open \(U\) (and
287	  its preimage \(V\)). The point that makes this replacement free is that the
288	  \(R\)-module structure on the sections is supplied \emph{uniformly} by
289	  restriction of scalars along the global-sections ring map at the top open,
290	  independently of the evaluation open; consequently both sides unwind to the same
291	  nested restriction-of-scalars towers, reconciled by applying the
292	  restriction-of-scalars composition identity twice and substituting the
293	  \(\top\)-level ring equation of
294	  Lemma~\ref{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}. Naturality in the open
295	  follows because every constituent of the construction is either a structure-sheaf
296	  restriction map or conjugation by a fixed ring map, and all of these commute with
297	  further restriction along an inclusion of opens.
298	
299	  \medskip
300	  \emph{Naturality of the family \(\{e_U\}_U\) in the open (remark).} The family is
301	  in fact a natural isomorphism between two presheaves of \(R\)-modules on
302	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\): the source presheaf
303	  \(U \mapsto \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N,\, U\bigr)\), with the
304	  structure-sheaf restriction maps of the pushforward, and the target presheaf
305	  \(U \mapsto
306	    \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(N,\, (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U)\bigr)\),
307	  with the structure-sheaf restriction maps of \(N\) transported through the preimage
308	  operation \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}\) and read as \(R\)-linear maps by
309	  restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\). Concretely, for any inclusion of opens
310	  \(U' \subseteq U\), with \(V = (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U\) and
311	  \(V' = (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U'\), the square with horizontal maps
312	  \(e_U, e_{U'}\) and vertical maps the respective structure-sheaf restrictions
313	  commutes. The reason is structural: each \(e_U\) is a composite whose constituents
314	  are of exactly two kinds — structure-sheaf restriction maps (of \(N\),
315	  respectively of \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N\)) and identity-on-carrier
316	  \(\operatorname{restrictScalars}\) repackagings (conjugation by the \emph{fixed},
317	  open-independent ring map supplied by the restriction-of-scalars reconciliation and
318	  by the \(\top\)-level ring equation of
319	  Lemma~\ref{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}). Restriction maps commute
320	  with further restriction by the presheaf functoriality axiom (using
321	  \(V' = (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U' \subseteq
322	  (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U = V\), as \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}\) is
323	  monotone), and conjugation by a fixed ring map commutes with restriction because the
324	  ring map does not depend on \(U\); the naturality square is the paste of these
325	  component squares, and each \(e_U\) being an isomorphism makes the family a natural
326	  isomorphism. This open-naturality is exactly the compatibility invoked, at the
327	  inclusion \(D(a) \subseteq \top\), in the proof of
328	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}.
329	\end{proof}
330	
331	\begin{lemma}
332	
333	\leanok
334	[The tilde restriction from the top open to a basic open is a localization]
335	  \label{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}
336	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}
337	  Let \(M\) be an \(R'\)-module and \(b \in R'\). Then the structure-sheaf
338	  restriction map
339	  \[
340	    \Gamma(\widetilde M,\, \top) \longrightarrow \Gamma(\widetilde M,\, D(b)),
341	  \]
342	  read as a map of \(R'\)-modules, exhibits \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, D(b))\) as the
343	  localization of \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(b)\). This is
344	  the \(R'\)-side localization fact transported in movement~(3) of the proof of
345	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}.
346	\end{lemma}
347	
348	\begin{proof}
349	
350	  By the defining property of \(\widetilde{(-)}\), the tilde-localization map
351	  \(M \to \Gamma(\widetilde M, D(b))\) exhibits its target as the localization of
352	  \(M\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(b)\). The global-sections map
353	  \(M \to \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\) is the localization of \(M\) at the trivial
354	  submonoid \(\mathrm{powers}(1)\) — since \(D(1) = \top\) — and is therefore
355	  bijective. The triangle identity relating these two localization maps to the
356	  structure-sheaf restriction \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, \top) \to
357	  \Gamma(\widetilde M, D(b))\) then exhibits the restriction itself, post-composed
358	  with the inverse of the bijection \(M \cong \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\), as the
359	  localization of \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(b)\).
360	\end{proof}
361	
362	We record three further bespoke supports that drive the basic-open verification of
363	the affine-pushforward isomorphism below. They carry no external citation and stand
364	on their proof sketches.
365	
366	\begin{lemma}
367	
368	\leanok
369	[Restriction of scalars of a localized module]
370	  \label{lem:powers_restrictScalars}
371	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars}
372	  Let \(A\) be a commutative \(R\)-algebra, \(S \subseteq R\) a submonoid, and
373	  \(f : M \to N\) an \(A\)-linear map that exhibits \(N\) as the localization of
374	  \(M\) at the image submonoid
375	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(S) \subseteq A\). Then the underlying \(R\)-linear map
376	  \(\operatorname{restr}_R f\) exhibits \(N\) as the localization of \(M\) at \(S\)
377	  itself.
378	\end{lemma}
379	
380	\begin{proof}
381	
382	
383	  This is the exact converse of the standard fact that a localization of \(M\) at a
384	  submonoid \(S\) of \(R\) remains a localization after enlarging the base to an
385	  \(R\)-algebra \(A\) along the image submonoid \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(S)\). One
386	  checks the three defining conditions of a localized module directly. Each
387	  \(s \in S\) acts invertibly on \(N\) because its image
388	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(s)\) does and the two actions coincide by the scalar
389	  tower \(R \to A \to N\). Surjectivity of \(f\) up to denominators and the
390	  equality-witness condition transport along the map
391	  \(s \mapsto \operatorname{algMap}_A(s)\) from \(S\) onto
392	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(S)\), again using
393	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(s)\cdot{} = s\cdot{}\). In the application
394	  \(A = R'\), \(S = \mathrm{powers}(a)\), one has
395	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_{R'}(\mathrm{powers}(a)) = \mathrm{powers}(\varphi a)\), so
396	  this identifies the localization of \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) at \(a\)
397	  with the localization of \(M\) at \(\varphi a\); it is the ring-change core of the
398	  basic-open computation below.
399	\end{proof}
400	
401	\begin{lemma}
402	
403	\leanok
404	[Section-level counit isomorphism from a localization hypothesis]
405	  \label{lem:fromTildeGamma_app_isIso_of_localized}
406	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule}
407	  Let \(N\) be an \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-module and \(a \in R\). Suppose the
408	  structure-sheaf restriction map \(\Gamma(N, \top) \to \Gamma(N, D(a))\), read as
409	  an \(R\)-linear map of the global-section modules, exhibits \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) as
410	  the localization of \(\Gamma(N, \top)\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\). Then the
411	  tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit
412	  \(\mathrm{fromTilde}\Gamma\,N : \widetilde{\Gamma(N,\top)} \to N\) restricts to an
413	  isomorphism on the sections over the basic open \(D(a)\).
414	\end{lemma}
415	
416	\begin{proof}
417	
418	
419	  Over \(D(a)\) the section map \(L\) of the counit sits in the triangle identity
420	  \(L \circ j = \rho\), where
421	  \(j : \Gamma(N,\top) \to \Gamma\bigl(\widetilde{\Gamma(N,\top)}, D(a)\bigr)\) is
422	  the tilde-localization map and \(\rho : \Gamma(N,\top) \to \Gamma(N, D(a))\) is
423	  the structure-sheaf restriction. Both \(j\) and \(\rho\) exhibit their targets as
424	  the localization of \(\Gamma(N,\top)\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\) — \(j\) by the
425	  defining property of \(\widetilde{(-)}\), and \(\rho\) by hypothesis. By the
426	  uniqueness of localized modules there is a unique \(R\)-linear isomorphism \(e\)
427	  with \(e \circ j = \rho\); the triangle identity then forces \(L = e\), so \(L\) is
428	  an isomorphism. (This is the per-basic-open input feeding the basis-local
429	  criterion Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis}.)
430	\end{proof}
431	
432	\begin{lemma}
433	
434	\leanok
435	[Affine pushforward of a tilde-module, conditional form]
436	  \label{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional}
437	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule}
438	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis, lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso,
439	  lem:fromTildeGamma_app_isIso_of_localized}
440	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and \(M\) an
441	  \(R'\)-module. Suppose that for every \(a \in R\) the structure-sheaf restriction
442	  of the pushforward \(N := (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M\) exhibits its
443	  sections over \(D(a)\) as the localization of its global sections at
444	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\). Then there is a canonical isomorphism of
445	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
446	  \[
447	    (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M \;\cong\;
448	    \widetilde{\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)} .
449	  \]
450	\end{lemma}
451	
452	\begin{proof}
453	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis, lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso, lem:fromTildeGamma_app_isIso_of_localized}
454	  Write \(N := (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M\). Under the hypothesis,
455	  Lemma~\ref{lem:fromTildeGamma_app_isIso_of_localized} applies for each \(a \in R\),
456	  so the counit \(\mathrm{fromTilde}\Gamma\,N\) restricts to an isomorphism on the
457	  sections over every basic open \(D(a)\). Since the basic opens form a basis of
458	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\), the basis-local criterion
459	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis} upgrades this to: the counit
460	  \(\mathrm{fromTilde}\Gamma\,N\) is an isomorphism, i.e.\ \(N\) lies in the
461	  essential image of \(\widetilde{(-)}\). Composing the inverse counit with the
462	  tilde of the global-sections comparison
463	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso} yields the asserted object isomorphism.
464	\end{proof}
465	
466	\begin{lemma}\leanok
467	[Affine pushforward of a tilde-module]
468	  \label{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
469	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
470	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis, lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso,
471	        lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional, lem:powers_restrictScalars,
472	        lem:gammaPushforwardIso, lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt,
473	        lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}
474	% NOTE: the carrier-wall obstruction to `hloc` is resolved (restriction-of-scalars /
475	% scalar-tower instances feed lem:powers_restrictScalars; lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule
476	% supplies the R'-side localization). The single remaining obligation is the open-naturality of
477	% the section comparison e_{D(a)}, established as a prose remark in the proof of
478	% lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt; the proof sketch below derives each per-a hloc(a) from the top-level
479	% localization plus that naturality square for the inclusion D(a) ⊆ ⊤, so no section-level square
480	% is hand-proved per a.
481	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let \(M\)
482	  be an \(R'\)-module. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of
483	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
484	  \[
485	    \bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\,\widetilde{M}
486	    \;\cong\;
487	    \widetilde{\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)},
488	  \]
489	  where \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi : \operatorname{Spec}(R') \to
490	  \operatorname{Spec}(R)\) is the induced morphism of affine schemes,
491	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\) denotes the quasi-coherent sheaf associated to a module, and
492	  \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) is the \(R\)-module obtained from \(M\) by
493	  restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\). That is, pushing the quasi-coherent
494	  sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\) forward along \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) is, up to
495	  this canonical identification, the tilde of the restriction of scalars of \(M\).
496	
497	  This is the classical fact that the direct image of a quasi-coherent sheaf along
498	  a morphism of affine schemes is computed on modules by restriction of scalars; it
499	  is the affine companion of the pullback formula (the affine case of the
500	  pushforward analogue of Stacks ``$\widetilde{M}$ and pullback''). It is recorded
501	  here as bespoke project infrastructure because the affine-pushforward
502	  identification does not appear as a ready-made result in the existing
503	  quasi-coherent sheaf theory.
504	\end{lemma}
505	
506	\begin{proof}
507	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis, lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso, lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional, lem:powers_restrictScalars, lem:gammaPushforwardIso, lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt, lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}
508	  We build the isomorphism \emph{directly on a basis of basic opens}. This is the
509	  non-circular route: checking the comparison map on basic opens yields the object
510	  isomorphism, and quasi-coherence of the pushforward is then a corollary, not a
511	  prerequisite. (One must not reverse this order: the global-sections comparison
512	  \(\Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde{M}\bigr) \cong
513	  \operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) of
514	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso} alone would only upgrade to an object
515	  isomorphism once the pushforward is already known to be quasi-coherent, so
516	  quasi-coherence cannot be deduced from an object isomorphism built that way.)
517	
518	  \emph{The comparison morphism.} There is a canonical morphism of
519	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
520	  \[
521	    \alpha : \widetilde{\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)}
522	    \longrightarrow \bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M},
523	  \]
524	  namely the tilde-adjoint of the global-sections identification of
525	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso}. We show \(\alpha\) is an isomorphism.
526	
527	  \medskip
528	  \emph{Reduction to basic opens.} The basic opens \(D(a)\), for \(a \in R\), form a
529	  basis of the topology of \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\). By the basis-local criterion
530	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis} it suffices to check that \(\alpha\)
531	  restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over each basic open \(D(a)\).
532	
533	  \medskip
534	  \emph{The computation over \(D(a)\).} On the source, the sections of
535	  \(\widetilde{(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M)}\) over \(D(a)\) are the
536	  localization \(\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)[1/a]\) as an
537	  \(R[1/a]\)-module. On the target, since \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}D(a) =
538	  D(\varphi a)\), the sections of \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*
539	  \widetilde{M}\) over \(D(a)\) are the sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over
540	  \(D(\varphi a)\), i.e.\ the localization \(M[1/\varphi a]\), regarded as an
541	  \(R[1/a]\)-module by restriction of scalars along the induced map
542	  \(R[1/a] \to R'[1/\varphi a]\). These two localizations agree: the element \(a\)
543	  acts on \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) through \(\varphi a\), so localizing
544	  \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) at the multiplicative set generated by \(a\) is
545	  the same module as localizing \(M\) at the multiplicative set generated by
546	  \(\varphi a\). Both are the universal localization of
547	  \(M\) inverting \(\varphi a\), so the comparison map \(\alpha\) is an isomorphism
548	  on the sections over \(D(a)\). As this holds for every basic open, \(\alpha\) is
549	  an isomorphism.
550	
551	  \medskip
552	  \emph{The explicit formalization route over \(D(a)\).} The agreement of the two
553	  localizations just described is the entire content, but it must be realized
554	  through the global ring \(R\), because the \(R\)-action on the pushforward
555	  sections over \(D(a)\) is built from the global-sections module of
556	  \(N := (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M\) rather than directly from
557	  \(R[1/a]\). The reduction therefore proceeds exactly as the conditional
558	  assembly Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional} prescribes: it
559	  suffices to supply, for each \(a \in R\), the hypothesis \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) that
560	  the structure-sheaf restriction \(\Gamma(N, \top) \to \Gamma(N, D(a))\) exhibits
561	  \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) as the localization of \(\Gamma(N, \top)\) at
562	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\). The \(R\)-action on \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) is the honest
563	  restriction-of-scalars action carried by the scalar tower \(R \to R' \to {-}\)
564	  determined by \(\varphi\); against that structure the ring-change converse
565	  Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} is applicable, so the carrier of the
566	  \(R\)-module is exactly the one to which the localization claim refers, and no
567	  recursively-defined or ad-hoc section-level scalar action intervenes.
568	  We obtain \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) by transporting the already-known \(R'\)-side
569	  localization (Lemma~\ref{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}), in
570	  element-free fashion, across the open-indexed section comparison of
571	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt} \emph{using its naturality in the open}
572	  (the naturality remark in the proof of
573	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}). This proceeds in three
574	  movements.
575	
576	  \emph{(1) The \(D(a)\)-level linear equivalence.} This is the component at
577	  \(U = D(a)\) of the open-indexed family of
578	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}: the \(R\)-linear isomorphism
579	  \[
580	    e_{D(a)} : \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M,\, D(a)\bigr)
581	      \;\cong\;
582	      \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(\widetilde M,\, D(\varphi a))\bigr) ,
583	  \]
584	  using \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}D(a) = D(\varphi a)\), which is
585	  definitional. No fresh \(D(a)\)-level construction is needed: this is precisely the
586	  open-indexed isomorphism already supplied by
587	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt} evaluated at the basic open \(D(a)\).
588	
589	  \emph{(2) The naturality square for \(D(a) \subseteq \top\).} The compatibility
590	  that relates \(e_{D(a)}\) to the \(\top\)-level isomorphism
591	  \(e_\top\) (the global-sections comparison of
592	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}) is precisely the open-naturality square of the
593	  family \(\{e_U\}_U\) (established in the proof of
594	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}), instantiated at the inclusion
595	  \(D(a) \subseteq \top\): the square
596	  \[
597	    \begin{array}{ccc}
598	      \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M,\, \top\bigr)
599	        & \xrightarrow{\;e_\top\;}
600	        & \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(\widetilde M,\, \top)\bigr) \\[4pt]
601	      {\scriptstyle \mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)}}\big\downarrow & &
602	        \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle \mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(\varphi a)}} \\[4pt]
603	      \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M,\, D(a)\bigr)
604	        & \xrightarrow{\;e_{D(a)}\;}
605	        & \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(\widetilde M,\, D(\varphi a))\bigr)
606	    \end{array}
607	  \]
608	  commutes, using \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}D(a) = D(\varphi a)\), which is
609	  definitional. Because the family \(\{e_U\}_U\) is a natural isomorphism, this single
610	  square is available uniformly in \(a\); there is no per-\(a\) section-level identity
611	  to re-prove by hand. It intertwines the source restriction
612	  \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)}\) on the pushforward with the (restriction-of-scalars
613	  reading of the) target restriction \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(\varphi a)}\) on
614	  \(\widetilde M\).
615	
616	  \emph{(3) Discharging \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) by transport.} The target restriction
617	  \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(\varphi a)} : \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top) \to
618	  \Gamma(\widetilde M, D(\varphi a))\) exhibits \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, D(\varphi a)) =
619	  M[1/\varphi a]\) as the \(\mathrm{powers}(\varphi a)\)-localization of
620	  \(M = \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\) over \(R'\); this is exactly
621	  Lemma~\ref{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}. Apply the ring-change converse
622	  Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} (with \(R\)-algebra \(A = R'\) and submonoid
623	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\), so that
624	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_{R'}(\mathrm{powers}(a)) = \mathrm{powers}(\varphi a)\)) —
625	  its \(\operatorname{Algebra} R\, R'\) and scalar-tower hypotheses being supplied by
626	  the restriction-of-scalars structure along \(\varphi\) — to read this same map as
627	  the \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\)-localization over \(R\). The commuting naturality square
628	  of movement (2) intertwines this target restriction with the source restriction
629	  \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)} : \Gamma(N, \top) \to \Gamma(N, D(a))\) by way of the
630	  isomorphisms \(e_\top\) and \(e_{D(a)}\); transporting the localization property
631	  across that square — via the transport-along-an-equivalence principle for localized
632	  modules — yields that \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)}\) is the
633	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\)-localization on \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) over \(R\), i.e.\
634	  \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\). No section-level computation specific to the individual \(a\)
635	  is performed: the only \(a\)-dependent input is the instantiation of the single
636	  natural-isomorphism square at \(D(a)\). Feeding the family
637	  \(\{\mathrm{hloc}(a)\}_{a \in R}\) to the conditional assembly
638	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional} produces the unconditional
639	  object isomorphism \(\alpha\).
640	
641	  \medskip
642	  \emph{Naturality in the open drives the transport.} The comparison assembled
643	  here, \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \circ \widetilde{(-)} \cong
644	  \widetilde{(-)} \circ \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\), is natural in \emph{both}
645	  arguments — in the module \(M\) and in the open \(U\). The naturality in the
646	  module is the usual functoriality; the naturality in the open is the naturality
647	  remark in the proof of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}: the family
648	  \(\{e_U\}_U\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt} commutes with the
649	  structure-sheaf restriction maps on both sides. It is precisely this
650	  open-naturality that drives the localization transport \emph{uniformly}: the
651	  per-\(a\) localization fact \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) follows from the \(\top\)-level
652	  localization Lemma~\ref{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule} together with that
653	  naturality square for the inclusion
654	  \(D(a) \hookrightarrow \top\), rather than from a section-level naturality square
655	  re-proved by hand for each individual \(a\). This is the single remaining
656	  obligation of the construction; the carrier of the \(R\)-action on the pushforward
657	  sections is the restriction-of-scalars structure along \(\varphi\), against which
658	  Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} applies directly.
659	
660	  The \(R\)-module structure on the \(R'\)-side sections to which the ring-change
661	  converse Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} is applied is the honest
662	  restriction-of-scalars structure: \(R\) acts through \(\varphi\), i.e.\ via the
663	  \(R\)-algebra structure on \(R'\) determined by \(\varphi\) and the associated
664	  scalar tower \(R \to R' \to M[1/\varphi a]\). It is \emph{not} an ad-hoc,
665	  recursively-defined scalar action. Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} is
666	  stated against exactly this restriction-of-scalars / scalar-tower structure, and
667	  it is under that structure that localizing \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) at
668	  \(a\) coincides with localizing \(M\) at \(\varphi a\).
669	
670	  % NOTE: This lemma is the affine case of the general fact that the pushforward of
671	  % a quasi-coherent sheaf along an affine morphism is quasi-coherent. In current
672	  % Mathlib master that fact is the IsLocalizing / fromTildeGamma characterization
673	  % (AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_fromTildeΓ_pushforward); it post-dates the project's
674	  % pinned Mathlib, so the project proves it in-tree by the basic-open route above.
675	  % The in-tree proof remains valid and is not made redundant by a future Mathlib
676	  % bump.
677	  \medskip
678	  \emph{Quasi-coherence as a corollary.} The object isomorphism just constructed
679	  exhibits \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M} \cong
680	  \widetilde{(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M)}\) as the tilde of a module. Tilde
681	  modules are quasi-coherent and quasi-coherence of \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
682	  is closed under isomorphism, so the pushforward
683	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M}\) is quasi-coherent. This
684	  conclusion is stated \emph{after} the object isomorphism, of which it is a
685	  consequence — no separate ``pushforward preserves quasi-coherent'' input is
686	  needed for the affine lane.
687	\end{proof}
688	
689	\begin{remark}
690	  This single isomorphism discharges both inputs that the affine reduction below
691	  requires of the pushforward of a tilde-module.
692	  \begin{enumerate}
693	    \item \emph{The global-sections comparison.} Applying the global-sections
694	      (module-of-sections) functor to the isomorphism identifies the
695	      \(R\)-module of sections of
696	      \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M}\) with
697	      \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\); this is exactly the \(\Gamma\)-fragment
698	      comparison used to recognise the section-level base-change map.
699	    \item \emph{Quasi-coherence of the pushforward.} The tilde of any module is
700	      quasi-coherent, and quasi-coherence of \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules is
701	      closed under isomorphism; hence
702	      \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M}\) is quasi-coherent.
703	      In particular no separate ``pushforward preserves quasi-coherent'' theorem is
704	      needed for the affine lane.
705	  \end{enumerate}
706	  Because both inputs the affine reduction asks of the pushforward are corollaries
707	  of this single object isomorphism — the section-level module identification and
708	  the quasi-coherence of the pushforward — this lemma is the sole bespoke
709	  ingredient the affine lane requires.
710	\end{remark}
711	
712	\subsection{The pullback companion}
713	
714	The affine reduction below requires, alongside the pushforward dictionary, the
715	\emph{pullback} companion: the affine description of the inverse image of a
716	tilde-module. It is the affine case of the classical fact that the pullback of a
717	quasi-coherent sheaf is computed on modules by base change of scalars.
718	
719	The pullback companion is built by uniqueness of left adjoints from the
720	per-object \(\Gamma\)-fragment comparison of
721	Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}, packaged as a natural isomorphism of
722	right-adjoint functors (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso}).
723	
724	\begin{lemma}
725	
726	\leanok
727	[Naturality packaging of the pushforward \(\Gamma\)-comparison]
728	  \label{lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso}
729	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardNatIso}
730	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
731	  The per-object comparison of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso} assembles into a
732	  natural isomorphism of the two right-adjoint functors
733	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \circ \widetilde{(-)}\) and
734	  \(\widetilde{(-)} \circ \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\) on modules, exhibiting the
735	  pushforward dictionary as a natural transformation rather than merely a family of
736	  object isomorphisms. This is the input to the uniqueness-of-left-adjoints argument
737	  that produces the pullback companion Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}.
738	\end{lemma}
739	\begin{proof}
740	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
741	  The components are the object isomorphisms of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso};
742	  naturality is the corresponding naturality square, which holds because every
743	  constituent of the comparison is identity-on-elements.
744	\end{proof}
745	
746	\begin{lemma}
747	
748	\leanok
749	[Affine pullback of a tilde-module]
750	  \label{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}
751	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullback_spec_tilde_iso}
752	  \uses{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso, lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso}
753	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Lemma `lemma-widetilde-pullback', Tag 01I9
754	  % (\S\,Quasi-coherent sheaves on affines) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex,
755	  % L1241--1256).
756	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let
757	  % $(X, \mathcal{O}_X) = (\Spec(S), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(S)})$,
758	  % $(Y, \mathcal{O}_Y) = (\Spec(R), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)})$
759	  % be affine schemes.
760	  % Let $\psi : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$ be a
761	  % morphism of affine schemes, corresponding to the ring map
762	  % $\psi^\sharp : R \to S$ (see Lemma \ref{lemma-category-affine-schemes}).
763	  % \begin{enumerate}
764	  % \item We have $\psi^* \widetilde M = \widetilde{S \otimes_R M}$
765	  % functorially in the $R$-module $M$.
766	  % \item We have $\psi_* \widetilde N = \widetilde{N_R}$ functorially
767	  % in the $S$-module $N$.
768	  % \end{enumerate}"
769	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, Lemma ``\(\widetilde M\) and pullback''
770	  (Tag 01I9), part (1).}
771	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let \(M\)
772	  be an \(R\)-module. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of
773	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R')\)-modules
774	  \[
775	    \bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)^{*}\,\widetilde{M}
776	    \;\cong\;
777	    \widetilde{\bigl(R' \otimes_R M\bigr)},
778	  \]
779	  where \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi : \operatorname{Spec}(R') \to
780	  \operatorname{Spec}(R)\) is the induced morphism of affine schemes,
781	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\) denotes the quasi-coherent sheaf associated to a module, and
782	  \(R' \otimes_R M\) is the base change of \(M\) along \(\varphi\) — the
783	  \(R'\)-module obtained by extension of scalars. The isomorphism is natural in
784	  \(M\). That is, pulling the quasi-coherent sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\) back along
785	  \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) is, up to this canonical identification, the tilde
786	  of the base change of \(M\). It is the pullback companion of the affine
787	  pushforward dictionary Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}, and is part~(1)
788	  of the same Stacks lemma of which the pushforward formula is part~(2).
789	\end{lemma}
790	
791	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The first assertion follows from the identification in
792	% Lemma \ref{lemma-compare-constructions}
793	% and the result of Modules, Lemma \ref{modules-lemma-restrict-quasi-coherent}."
794	\begin{proof}
795	  \uses{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
796	  Pullback along \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) is, on quasi-coherent sheaves, the

635	    -- app, and `restrictScalars.map_apply` are all `rfl`; the middle `restrictScalarsCongr` (now
636	    -- replaces the former `eqToIso`, removing the only non-`rfl` cast) merely repackages the module
637	    -- structure on the unchanged carrier. So both legs of the square send `x` to the underlying
638	    -- `tForget x` definitionally — a pointwise `rfl` closes it, sidestepping the rewrite-matching
639	    -- pathology that defeated the prior `restrictScalarsComp'App_inv_naturality`-rewrite route.
640	    ext x
641	    rfl
642	  -- Solve for `ρ` and transport the localization across the two equivalences.
643	  have hρ : ρ = (e₁.hom ≫ Gmor) ≫ e₂.inv := (Iso.eq_comp_inv e₂).mpr hsq
644	  have key : ρ.hom
645	      = e₂.toLinearEquiv.symm.toLinearMap ∘ₗ
646	          (Gmor.hom ∘ₗ e₁.toLinearEquiv.toLinearMap) := by
647	    rw [hρ]; rfl
648	  rw [key]
649	  haveI hstep : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
650	      (Gmor.hom ∘ₗ e₁.toLinearEquiv.toLinearMap) :=
651	    IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (Submonoid.powers a) Gmor.hom e₁.toLinearEquiv
652	  exact IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv (Submonoid.powers a)
653	    (Gmor.hom ∘ₗ e₁.toLinearEquiv.toLinearMap) e₂.toLinearEquiv.symm

655	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — affine pullback dictionary -/
656	
657	/-- **Naturality of the Γ-fragment comparison.** The per-object isomorphisms
658	`gammaPushforwardIso φ N : Γ_R((Spec φ)_* N) ≅ restrictScalars φ (Γ_{R'} N)` assemble into a
659	natural isomorphism of functors `(Spec R').Modules ⥤ ModuleCat R`
660	\[ (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \;\circ\; \Gamma_R
661	   \;\cong\; \Gamma_{R'} \;\circ\; \operatorname{restr}_\varphi . \]
662	Every constituent of `gammaPushforwardIso` is the identity on underlying elements (the
663	`restrictScalarsComp'App` isos and `restrictScalarsCongr` merely repackage the module structure
664	on the unchanged carrier), so naturality is a pointwise `rfl`. This is the right-adjoint natural
665	isomorphism that drives the affine pullback dictionary `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` via
666	`Adjunction.natIsoOfRightAdjointNatIso`. Project-local. -/
667	noncomputable def gammaPushforwardNatIso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R') :
668	    Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ) ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R) ≅
669	      moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R') ⋙ ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom :=
670	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun N => gammaPushforwardIso φ N) (by
671	    intro N N' g
672	    ext x
673	    rfl)
674	
675	/-- **Affine pullback of a tilde-module.** For a ring map `φ : R ⟶ R'` and an `R`-module `M`,
676	pulling the quasi-coherent sheaf `M^~` back along `Spec φ` is, up to canonical isomorphism, the
677	tilde of the extension of scalars `R' ⊗_R M`. This is the pullback companion of
678	`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` and is part (1) of Stacks Tag 01I9 (`lemma-widetilde-pullback`).
679	
680	The construction is the uniqueness-of-left-adjoints route. Pullback along `Spec φ` is left adjoint
681	to pushforward, and `tilde` is left adjoint to global sections; their composite
682	`tilde_R ⋙ pullback` is left adjoint to `pushforward ⋙ Γ_R`. Symmetrically `extendScalars φ` is
683	left adjoint to `restrictScalars φ`, so `extendScalars ⋙ tilde_{R'}` is left adjoint to
684	`Γ_{R'} ⋙ restrictScalars φ`. The two right adjoints are identified by the natural isomorphism
685	`gammaPushforwardNatIso` (which is the global-sections comparison `gammaPushforwardIso`), so
686	`Adjunction.natIsoOfRightAdjointNatIso` yields an isomorphism of the two left adjoints; evaluating
687	at `M` gives the claim. Project-local: the affine companion of the pushforward dictionary. See
688	blueprint `lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso`. -/
689	noncomputable def pullback_spec_tilde_iso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}}
690	    (φ : R ⟶ R') (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) :
691	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
692	      tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars φ.hom).obj M) :=
693	  let adjL := (tilde.adjunction (R := R)).comp
694	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map φ))
695	  let adjR := (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj φ.hom).comp (tilde.adjunction (R := R'))
696	  (((conjugateIsoEquiv adjL adjR).symm (gammaPushforwardNatIso φ)).symm).app M
697	
698	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — pullback cone legs as Spec of tensor inclusions -/
699	
700	/-- **The pullback cone legs are the `Spec`-maps of the tensor inclusions.** For ring maps
701	`ψ : R ⟶ R'` and `φ : R ⟶ A` (regarding `A`, `R'` as `R`-algebras via `φ`, `ψ`), over the generic
702	pullback square `Limits.pullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)` Mathlib's `pullbackSpecIso` identifies
703	the total space with `Spec (A ⊗[R] R')`, and under it the two cone legs are the `Spec`-maps of the
704	canonical tensor inclusions `Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom : A → A ⊗[R] R'` and
705	`Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight : R' → A ⊗[R] R'`. The only content beyond Mathlib's
706	`pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst`/`_inv_snd` is the structure bridging `Spec.map φ ↔ Spec.map (algebraMap
707	R A)` (definitional once `A` carries the `φ`-algebra structure). See blueprint
708	`lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor`. -/
709	theorem pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}} (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) :
710	    letI : Algebra R A := φ.hom.toAlgebra
711	    letI : Algebra R R' := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
712	    ((pullbackSpecIso (R := ↑R) (S := ↑A) (T := ↑R')).inv ≫
713	        Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
714	        = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom)) ∧
715	      ((pullbackSpecIso (R := ↑R) (S := ↑A) (T := ↑R')).inv ≫
716	        Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
717	        = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
718	          (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight (R := ↑R) (A := ↑A) (B := ↑R')).toRingHom)) := by
719	  letI : Algebra R A := φ.hom.toAlgebra
720	  letI : Algebra R R' := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
721	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
722	  · exact pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst ↑R ↑A ↑R'
723	  · exact pullbackSpecIso_inv_snd ↑R ↑A ↑R'
724	
725	/-! ## Section-level mate computation, decomposed -/
726	
727	/-- **Domain read of the section-level base-change map.** In the affine–affine model
728	(`g = Spec ψ`, `f = Spec φ`, `F = tilde M`), the global sections of the domain
729	`g^*(f_*(tilde M))` of the base-change map are canonically `R' ⊗_R M` as an `R'`-module — i.e.
730	the extension of scalars along `ψ` of the restriction of scalars along `φ` of `M`. The iso
731	`Θ_src` is the composite of the pushforward dictionary `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` (reading
732	`f_*(tilde M)` as `restrictScalars φ M`) followed by the pullback dictionary
733	`pullback_spec_tilde_iso` (reading `g^*` of a tilde as extension of scalars along `ψ`), then the
734	tilde–Γ unit. The cospan maps `f = Spec φ`, `g = Spec ψ` are genuine `Spec`-maps, so the two
735	dictionaries apply directly with no pullback-leg identification needed. See blueprint
736	`lem:base_change_mate_domain_read`. -/

749	/-- **Pullback of modules along an isomorphism of schemes is an equivalence.** Project-local

758	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (inv f) f).symm)
759	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f (inv f) ≪≫
760	      Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (IsIso.hom_inv_id f) ≪≫ Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X)

766	/-- **Codomain read of the section-level base-change map.** In the affine–affine model, with

773	noncomputable def base_change_mate_codomain_read {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
774	    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :

776	    letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
777	    (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).obj
778	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))).obj
779	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))).obj
780	            (tilde M))) ≅
781	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars
782	          (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u))
783	            (B := (R' : Type u))).toRingHom).obj
784	        ((ModuleCat.extendScalars
785	          (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u))
786	            (B := (R' : Type u)))).obj M) := by
787	  letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (A : Type u) := φ.hom.toAlgebra
788	  letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
789	  set e := pullbackSpecIso (R := (R : Type u)) (S := (A : Type u)) (T := (R' : Type u)) with he
790	  set inclA := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
791	    (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u)) (B := (R' : Type u))) with hinclA
792	  set inclR' := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight
793	    (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u)) (B := (R' : Type u))).toRingHom with hinclR'
794	  obtain ⟨hfst0, hsnd0⟩ := pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor ψ φ
795	  have hfst : Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ) = e.hom ≫ Spec.map inclA :=
796	    (Iso.inv_comp_eq e).mp hfst0
797	  have hsnd : Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ) = e.hom ≫ Spec.map inclR' :=
798	    (Iso.inv_comp_eq e).mp hsnd0
799	  set g' := Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ) with hg'
800	  set f' := Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ) with hf'
801	  set W₀ := (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map inclA)).obj (tilde M) with hW₀
802	  -- Read the pullback leg `g'` through `pullbackSpecIso` as the affine `Spec inclA` pullback.
803	  have iso_g : (Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj (tilde M)
804	        ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback e.hom).obj W₀ :=
805	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hfst).app (tilde M) ≪≫
806	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp e.hom (Spec.map inclA)).symm.app (tilde M)
807	  -- `e.hom` is an iso of schemes, so pulling back then pushing forward returns the module.

809	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback e.hom).obj W₀) :=
810	    (asIso (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction e.hom).unit).app W₀
811	  -- Assemble the object isomorphism `C₀ ≅ tilde ((A⊗R')⊗_A M)`.
812	  refine (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).mapIso ?_ ≪≫
813	    (tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars inclR'.hom).obj
814	      ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M))).symm
815	  exact (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f').mapIso iso_g ≪≫
816	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr hsnd).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback e.hom).obj W₀) ≪≫
817	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp e.hom (Spec.map inclR')).symm.app
818	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback e.hom).obj W₀) ≪≫
819	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map inclR')).mapIso unit_iso.symm ≪≫
820	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map inclR')).mapIso (pullback_spec_tilde_iso inclA M) ≪≫
821	    pushforward_spec_tilde_iso inclR' ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)

823	/-- **Regrouping isomorphism for the section-level mate (object form).** The bundled `R'`-linear

825	\[ (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M \;\cong\; R' \otimes_R M \]

854	noncomputable def base_change_mate_regroupEquiv {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}

858	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars
859	        (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u))
860	          (B := (R' : Type u))).toRingHom).obj
861	      ((ModuleCat.extendScalars
862	        (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u))
863	          (B := (R' : Type u)))).obj M) ≅
864	      (ModuleCat.extendScalars ψ.hom).obj ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M) := by

992	The proof is the blueprint's three-step adjoint-mate trace: (1) the value of the `((g')^*,(g')_*)`-unit
993	`η'` on the global module `M` is the base-change unit `m ↦ (1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`; (2) applying `f_* =

1000	theorem base_change_mate_generator_trace_eq {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
1001	    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
1002	    (base_change_mate_domain_read ψ φ M).inv ≫
1003	      (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map
1004	        (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
1005	          (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
1006	          (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
1007	          (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w (tilde M)) ≫
1008	      (base_change_mate_codomain_read ψ φ M).hom

1075	  rings, and regard \(R'\) and \(A\) as \(R\)-algebras via \(\psi\) and \(\varphi\).
1076	  Over the generic pullback square
1077	  \(\operatorname{Limits.pullback}(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi, \operatorname{Spec}\psi)\)
1078	  the canonical isomorphism \texttt{pullbackSpecIso}
1079	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackSpecIso_mathlib}) identifies the total space with
1080	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R' \otimes_R A)\), and under it the two cone legs are the
1081	  \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-maps of the canonical tensor inclusions:
1082	  \[
1083	    \operatorname{pullback.fst} \;=\; (\texttt{pullbackSpecIso}).\mathrm{hom}^{-1}
1084	      \circ \operatorname{Spec}\bigl(A \to R' \otimes_R A\bigr),
1085	    \qquad
1086	    \operatorname{pullback.snd} \;=\; (\texttt{pullbackSpecIso}).\mathrm{hom}^{-1}
1087	      \circ \operatorname{Spec}\bigl(R' \to R' \otimes_R A\bigr),
1088	  \]
1089	  the inclusions being \(a \mapsto 1 \otimes a\) and \(r' \mapsto r' \otimes 1\)
1090	  (equivalently, the \texttt{\_hom\_fst} / \texttt{\_hom\_snd} / \texttt{\_inv\_fst}
1091	  forms of those Mathlib identities).

1095	  \uses{lem:pullbackSpecIso_mathlib}
1096	  This is Mathlib's \texttt{pullbackSpecIso\_hom\_fst} and
1097	  \texttt{pullbackSpecIso\_hom\_snd} (and their \texttt{\_inv} companions) transported
1098	  to the present square. The only content beyond citing those identities is the
1099	  structure bridging: the given morphisms \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) and
1100	  \(\operatorname{Spec}\psi\) must be matched with the \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-maps of
1101	  the \texttt{algebraMap}s for the \(R\)-algebra structures that \(\varphi\) and
1102	  \(\psi\) define on \(A\) and \(R'\) — a definitional identification of
1103	  \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) with
1104	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(\texttt{CommRingCat.ofHom}\,(\texttt{algebraMap}\,R\,A))\) (and
1105	  likewise for \(\psi\)), after which the Mathlib companion lemmas apply verbatim. This
1106	  identification of the cone legs is the one Mathlib-absent step of the section-level
1107	  computation; isolating it here exposes it as a small, self-contained obligation.
1108	\end{proof}

1218	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1219	[Pullback along an isomorphism is an equivalence of module categories]
1220	  \label{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1221	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1222	  Let \(f : X \to Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes. Then the pullback functor on
1223	  \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules \(f^* : Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \to X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\) is
1224	  one half of an equivalence of categories
1225	  \(Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \simeq X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\), with quasi-inverse
1226	  \((f^{-1})^*\).
1227	\end{lemma}
1228	\begin{proof}
1229	  Assemble the equivalence from the functor \(f^*\) and the candidate inverse
1230	  \((f^{-1})^*\). The unit and counit isomorphisms are built from the pseudofunctor
1231	  coherences of pullback: \((f^{-1})^* \circ f^* \cong (f \circ f^{-1})^* = \mathrm{id}^*
1232	  \cong \mathrm{id}\) and symmetrically, using
1233	  \(f \circ f^{-1} = \mathrm{id}_Y\), \(f^{-1} \circ f = \mathrm{id}_X\)
1234	  (composition-of-pullbacks, congruence under equal maps, and pullback-of-identity).
1235	  The triangle identities are forced by these coherences.
1236	\end{proof}
1237	
1238	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1239	[The pullback functor along an isomorphism is an equivalence]
1240	  \label{lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}
1241	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}
1242	  \uses{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1243	  Consequently, for an isomorphism \(f : X \to Y\) the functor
1244	  \(f^* : Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \to X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\) is an equivalence of
1245	  categories. In particular its adjunction unit is a natural isomorphism, so
1246	  \((\eta_{f})_{W}\) is invertible on every object \(W\).
1247	\end{lemma}
1248	\begin{proof}
1249	  The functor underlying the equivalence of
1250	  \cref{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso} is exactly \(f^*\), so \(f^*\) is an
1251	  equivalence; an equivalence's unit is a natural isomorphism.
1252	\end{proof}

1258	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1259	[Regrouping isomorphism for the section-level mate]
1260	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1261	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1262	  % NOTE (iter-003): tensor-order convention. The Lean uses Mathlib's `pullbackSpecIso`
1263	  % orientation `A ⊗[R] R'` (so the codomain is `(A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M` and the generator
1264	  % goes `r' ⊗ m ↦ (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`); the prose below writes `R' ⊗_R A`. The two are the
1265	  % same module up to `TensorProduct.comm` — a faithful re-orientation, no content change.
1266	  \uses{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv, lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
1267	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
1268	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
1269	  % "boils down to the equality" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
1270	  % L933--938).
1271	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the
1272	  % equality
1273	  % $$
1274	  % (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M
1275	  % $$
1276	  % as $R'$-modules."
1277	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
1278	  change'' (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
1279	  Let \(\psi : R \to R'\) and \(\varphi : R \to A\) be ring homomorphisms and \(M\) an
1280	  \(A\)-module. There is a bundled \(R'\)-linear isomorphism
1281	  \[
1282	    \operatorname{regroup} :
1283	    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M ,
1284	  \]
1285	  the ``equality as \(R'\)-modules'' of the source, sending the generator
1286	  \((r' \otimes 1) \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes m\); equivalently its inverse sends
1287	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\). It carries \emph{no} flatness
1288	  hypothesis.
1289	\end{lemma}
1290	
1291	\begin{proof}
1292	  \uses{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv, lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
1293	  The underlying \(R'\)-linear equivalence is supplied, already as a bundled
1294	  \(\operatorname{LinearEquiv}\) over \(R'\), by the standalone geometry-free helper
1295	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}, whose construction is now the
1296	  natively \(R'\)-linear pushout-cancellation equivalence
1297	  \(\operatorname{Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange}\)
1298	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}). Crucially, that helper bundles
1299	  \(R'\)-linearity \emph{internally}: there is no hand-written scalar-compatibility
1300	  obligation, no induction over elementary tensors, and in particular \emph{no} residual
1301	  \(r' \cdot 0 = 0\) bookkeeping branch. The only work left to this proof is therefore to
1302	  promote that \(R'\)-linear equivalence to a morphism of \(R'\)-module \emph{objects} in
1303	  the exact \(\operatorname{extendScalars}/\operatorname{restrictScalars}\) packaging that
1304	  the codomain and domain reads produce. We work in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation
1305	  (the same module as \(R' \otimes_R A\) up to the canonical commutativity isomorphism,
1306	  per the tensor-order convention note).
1307	
1308	  \emph{The lone residual obstruction is the \(A\)-action diamond, resolved at the object
1309	  level.} The pushout-cancellation helper takes its relative tensor product over the
1310	  \emph{canonical} \(A\)-algebra structure on \(A \otimes_R R'\)
1311	  (\(\operatorname{Algebra.TensorProduct.leftAlgebra}\), multiplication in the left
1312	  factor), whereas the carrier object produced by the dictionaries,
1313	  \((\operatorname{extendScalars}\iota)\,M\) with \(\iota : A \to A \otimes_R R'\) the
1314	  left inclusion, tensors over the \(\operatorname{restr}_\iota\) \(A\)-action arising
1315	  from \(\iota.\mathrm{toAlgebra}\). These two \(A\)-module structures on
1316	  \(A \otimes_R R'\) agree on elements but are distinct as data, so the two relative
1317	  tensor products are distinct types even though their underlying abelian groups and
1318	  their \(R'\)-actions coincide. This diamond is the \emph{only} thing the helper does
1319	  not already discharge, and — because the \(R'\)-linearity is now entirely internal to
1320	  the Mathlib equivalence — it is resolved purely at the carrier level by an
1321	  identity-on-elements \(R'\)-linear bridge
1322	  \[
1323	    eT : (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\operatorname{restr}} M
1324	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1325	    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\mathrm{can}} M ,
1326	  \]
1327	  the identity map on generators, which is \(R'\)-linear because both sides carry the
1328	  same \(R'\)-action (restriction of scalars along \(R' \to A \otimes_R R'\)).
1329	
1330	  Composing the bridge \(eT\) with the natively \(R'\)-linear helper equivalence and
1331	  promoting along \(\operatorname{LinearEquiv.toModuleIso}\) yields the desired bundled
1332	  \(R'\)-linear isomorphism of \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(R')\) objects. Being a composite
1333	  of linear equivalences it is an \(R'\)-linear isomorphism, and none of its constituents
1334	  carries a flatness hypothesis.
1335	\end{proof}

1409	\begin{proof}
1410	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:base_change_mate_domain_read,
1411	        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1412	  The argument is carried out \emph{directly on the module of global sections}; no
1413	  abstract adjoint-mate identification at the level of sheaves intervenes. By
1414	  hom-extensionality for \(R'\)-linear maps it suffices to check the asserted equality on
1415	  the \(R'\)-module generators \(r' \otimes m\) of the source \(R' \otimes_R M\), and by
1416	  \(R'\)-linearity on the generators \(1 \otimes m\).
1417	
1418	  \emph{The base-change map is the base change of the unit.} For a quasi-coherent sheaf
1419	  on an affine scheme the base-change comparison \(\theta\) is, by construction, the
1420	  pullback–pushforward comparison obtained by applying \(g^*\) to the unit of the
1421	  \(\bigl((g')^*, (g')_*\bigr)\)-adjunction; read on global sections through the two tilde
1422	  dictionaries this is precisely the extension of scalars along \(\psi : R \to R'\) of the
1423	  algebraic unit of extension of scalars along \(A \to A \otimes_R R'\). Concretely, the
1424	  unit \(\eta_M : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\) sends \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1)
1425	  \otimes m\) (the codomain read identifies \(\Gamma\bigl(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\bigr)\)
1426	  with \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}),
1427	  and the domain read identifies the source with \(R' \otimes_R M\)
1428	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}). The base-change map therefore is the
1429	  \(R'\)-linearization \(\operatorname{LinearMap.lTensor} R'\,\eta_M\) of \(\eta_M\)
1430	  evaluated through the \(R'\)-action on the target: on the generator \(r' \otimes m\),
1431	  \[
1432	    r' \otimes m \;\longmapsto\; r' \cdot \eta_M(m)
1433	    \;=\; r' \cdot \bigl((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\bigr)
1434	    \;=\; (1 \otimes r') \otimes m ,
1435	  \]
1436	  using that the \(R'\)-action on \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\) is restriction of
1437	  scalars along \(R' \to A \otimes_R R'\), \(r' \mapsto 1 \otimes r'\).
1438	
1439	  \emph{Identification with \(\operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\).} The regrouping isomorphism
1440	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv} has inverse sending
1441	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\) on generators. This is the same
1442	  assignment as the displayed map above, and both maps are \(R'\)-linear, so they
1443	  coincide. This is exactly the ``boils down to the equality''
1444	  \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M\) of the source, read as an identity of
1445	  \(R'\)-modules; the regrouping isomorphism realizes the equality and the base-change map
1446	  is its inverse. In particular the conjugated map is an isomorphism, with no flatness
1447	  hypothesis used anywhere.
1448	\end{proof}

1755	\section{Flat base change for the pushforward}
1756	
1757	The global statement is assembled from the affine lemma and the single homological
1758	input that flatness preserves the finite equalizer computing \(H^0\). That input is
1759	supplied verbatim by Mathlib, recorded here as a dependency anchor.

1761	\begin{lemma}[Flat base change preserves finite equalizers]
1762	  \label{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
1763	  \lean{LinearMap.tensorEqLocusEquiv}
1764	  \mathlibok
1765	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1766	  Let \(A \to B\) be a flat ring homomorphism (more generally, let \(B\) be a flat
1767	  \(A\)-module that is also an \(A\)-algebra, with the scalar-tower compatibility),
1768	  and let \(f, g : N \rightrightarrows P\) be a pair of \(A\)-linear maps. Write
1769	  \(\operatorname{eq}(f, g) = \{x \in N : f(x) = g(x)\}\) for their equalizer. Then the
1770	  canonical \(B\)-linear comparison map
1771	  \[
1772	    B \otimes_A \operatorname{eq}(f, g)
1773	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1774	    \operatorname{eq}\bigl(\mathbf{1}_B \otimes f,\ \mathbf{1}_B \otimes g\bigr)
1775	  \]
1776	  is an isomorphism; that is, base change along a flat algebra commutes with the

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Regrouping isomorphism — pure-tensor-algebra core
10	
11	This file contains `AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_regroup_linearEquiv`, the
12	axiom-clean `R'`-linear equivalence `(A ⊗[R] R') ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[R'] R' ⊗[R] M`
13	that is the mathematical heart of the section-level flat-base-change mate.
14	
15	It lives in a separate compilation unit so that the `Module A (A ⊗[R] R')`
16	instance diamond (between `Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra` and the
17	`extendScalars`/`restrictScalars` canonical structure) normalises at the
18	import boundary, enabling `FlatBaseChange.lean` to close its
19	`base_change_mate_regroupEquiv` `map_smul'` with the one-liner
20	`exact LinearEquiv.toModuleIso (base_change_regroup_linearEquiv ↑M)`.
21	
22	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_RegroupHelper.tex`.
23	-/
24	
25	set_option autoImplicit false
26	
27	universe u
28	
29	open scoped TensorProduct
30	
31	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
32	
33	/-! ## Regrouping isomorphism for the section-level mate (L4-a) -/
34	
35	/-- **Regrouping isomorphism, pure-tensor-algebra core.** For ring homomorphisms `ψ : R → R'`,
36	`φ : R → A` (giving `A` the `R`-algebra structure) and an `A`-module `M` (with `M` an `R`-module
37	via `φ` and `IsScalarTower R A M`), there is a bundled `R'`-linear isomorphism
38	\[ (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M, \]
39	where `R'` acts on the source through the `right`-algebra structure on `A ⊗[R] R'`
40	(`Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra`, i.e. the `includeRight` factor). On the generator it sends
41	`(1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m ↦ r' ⊗ m`; its inverse sends `r' ⊗ m ↦ (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`. It carries **no** flatness
42	hypothesis.
43	
44	The construction is exactly the blueprint composite
45	`comm ≪≫ cancelBaseChange ≪≫ comm` (in the `A ⊗_R R'` orientation):
46	\[ (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M \xrightarrow{\texttt{comm}} M \otimes_A (A \otimes_R R')
47	   \xrightarrow{\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}} M \otimes_R R'
48	   \xrightarrow{\texttt{comm}} R' \otimes_R M, \]
49	assembled at the additive level from Mathlib's `TensorProduct.comm` (×2) and
50	`TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange` (instantiated at the tower `R → A → A` with
51	module `M` and base-change object `R'`), then re-bundled as an `R'`-linear equivalence by verifying
52	`R'`-`map_smul` on generators (`cancelBaseChange_tmul` plus the `rightAlgebra` action
53	`r' • (a ⊗ s) = a ⊗ (r' * s)`). This is the **mathematical heart** of the section-level mate
54	identification (`lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv`); the object-level packaging needed by the
55	chain is `base_change_mate_regroupEquiv` below. Axiom-clean (`propext`, `Quot.sound`).
56	
57	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma "Affine base change", proof, the
58	"boils down to the equality `(R' ⊗_R A) ⊗_A M = R' ⊗_R M`" step. -/
59	noncomputable def base_change_regroup_linearEquiv
60	    {R A R' : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [CommRing R']
61	    [Algebra R A] [Algebra R R'] (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module A M]
62	    [IsScalarTower R A M] :
63	    letI : Algebra R' (A ⊗[R] R') := Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra
64	    ((A ⊗[R] R') ⊗[A] M) ≃ₗ[R'] (R' ⊗[R] M) := by
65	  letI : Algebra R' (A ⊗[R] R') := Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra
66	  -- The underlying additive composite `comm ≫ cancelBaseChange ≫ comm`.
67	  let g : ((A ⊗[R] R') ⊗[A] M) ≃+ (R' ⊗[R] M) :=
68	    (TensorProduct.comm A (A ⊗[R] R') M).toAddEquiv.trans
69	      (((TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange R A A M R').toAddEquiv).trans
70	        (TensorProduct.comm R M R').toAddEquiv)
71	  refine { g with map_smul' := ?_ }
72	  intro r' x
73	  simp only [AddEquiv.toFun_eq_coe, RingHom.id_apply]
74	  induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
75	  | zero => simp
76	  | add a b ha hb => rw [smul_add, map_add, map_add, smul_add, ha, hb]
77	  | tmul t m =>
78	    induction t using TensorProduct.induction_on with
79	    | zero => simp
80	    | add a b ha hb => rw [TensorProduct.add_tmul, smul_add, map_add, map_add, smul_add, ha, hb]
81	    | tmul a s =>
82	      -- `r' • ((a ⊗ s) ⊗ m) = (a ⊗ (r' * s)) ⊗ m` via the `rightAlgebra` action on `A ⊗[R] R'`.
83	      have hsmul : r' • ((a ⊗ₜ[R] s) ⊗ₜ[A] m) = (a ⊗ₜ[R] (r' * s)) ⊗ₜ[A] m := by
84	        rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
85	        congr 1
86	        rw [Algebra.smul_def, show (algebraMap R' (A ⊗[R] R')) r' = (1 : A) ⊗ₜ[R] r' from rfl,
87	          Algebra.TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul]
88	        simp
89	      rw [hsmul]
90	      change (TensorProduct.comm R M R') (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange
91	          R A A M R' ((TensorProduct.comm A (A ⊗[R] R') M) ((a ⊗ₜ[R] (r' * s)) ⊗ₜ[A] m)))
92	        = r' • g ((a ⊗ₜ[R] s) ⊗ₜ[A] m)
93	      show _ = r' • (TensorProduct.comm R M R') (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange
94	          R A A M R' ((TensorProduct.comm A (A ⊗[R] R') M) ((a ⊗ₜ[R] s) ⊗ₜ[A] m)))
95	      simp only [TensorProduct.comm_tmul, TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange_tmul,
96	        TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
97	      rw [smul_eq_mul]
98	
99	end AlgebraicGeometry

      (M := (M : Type u)))
